body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: no

## Reasoning

Claude Code natively supports backgrounded shell processes via the Bash tool's `run_in_background: true` parameter (and the corresponding BashOutput tool to poll their output). The agent surface is real: `Bash run_in_background` spawns a detached child process and returns immediately, the turn closes at `end_turn`, and the user can ask Claude to follow up later via BashOutput.

irrlicht's transport, however, is fundamentally JSONL-transcript-driven for claudecode (`FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir(), JSONLineParser}` in agent.go:36). A backgrounded shell process has NO transcript of its own — it's a raw child process under the `claude` parent, indistinguishable from any other shell command Claude has spawned. The Process matcher is `ExactName{Name: "claude"}` (agent.go:33), which matches only the `claude` executable itself; the backgrounded shell child is not matched as a session.

Mapped to spec bullets — what irrlicht CANNOT see today:

- 'Agent stays working while the background process is alive, even after the turn's normal completion signal' — the parent transcript will emit `end_turn` (terminal stop_reason) when the Bash tool returns, and `resolveAssistantStreaming` (parser.go:288) will let `IsAgentDone()` fire, taking the agent to `ready`. The shell child's continued existence is invisible to the JSONL tailer.
- 'BackgroundProcessCount > 0 while any background process is alive' — no such counter exists on `SessionMetrics`. The parser does not introspect `Bash` tool_use inputs for `run_in_background: true`, and even if it did, there is no liveness signal — only future BashOutput tool calls reference the process by ID, and those only fire if the user/agent explicitly polls.
- 'When the last background process exits: agent → ready' — moot, because the agent never went into the extended `working` window in the first place.

In principle the parser could be extended to (a) snapshot the `bash_id` from a `Bash` tool_use whose input includes `run_in_background: true`, (b) maintain a per-session BackgroundProcessCount that increments on spawn and decrements on a BashOutput showing a terminated status or on a KillShell tool_use, and (c) hold the session in `working` while the count > 0. But none of this exists today, and the implementation is non-trivial because Claude Code does not actively report when a background shell exits — only when the agent or user polls it via BashOutput. A proper liveness signal would likely require either a hook (PostBackgroundExit, which doesn't exist upstream) or process-tree walking from the `claude` PID.

This is the only one of the 5 cells in this batch where the gap is in irrlicht's transport rather than upstream. It is a candidate for a follow-up issue but is out of scope for the current scenario matrix.
caveats: agent_supports is yes — `Bash run_in_background: true` plus BashOutput / KillShell is real, documented behavior in current Claude Code.

irrlicht_observes is no because (1) there is no irrlicht counter for background shell processes, (2) the parser does not parse Bash tool_use inputs for the run_in_background flag, and (3) there is no upstream liveness signal — Claude Code only reports background-process exit on demand via BashOutput, not as an event the tailer can observe asynchronously.

A future enhancement would extend the parser with a BackgroundProcessTracker that snapshots Bash tool_use entries with run_in_background, increments a metric, and decrements on BashOutput tool_result lines that report a terminated status. Holding the session in `working` would need a SubagentCounter-style hook (BackgroundProcessCounter) so the IsAgentDone path can consider the count. This is a real but non-trivial piece of work — file an issue if the scenario matrix promotes this cell to required coverage.

Recording the recipe is not blocked — we can ship a fixture that prompts Claude to `Bash run_in_background: true` and asserts the (current) buggy behavior: agent goes ready while a process is still alive. The recording would document the gap rather than validate spec compliance.

sources.note: Process.Match = ExactName{Name: "claude"} (line 33) — only the parent claude binary is tracked as a session. Backgrounded shell children spawned by Bash run_in_background are NOT matched.

No BackgroundProcessCounter exists. ParsedEvent has no BackgroundProcessCount field. resolveAssistantStreaming (line 288) lets end_turn fire normally as soon as the Bash tool_use returns, regardless of whether the spawned process is still alive.
Spec requires agent to stay `working` while any background process is alive and BackgroundProcessCount > 0 — neither observable exists in current irrlicht transport.
